items.title: Regeni verdict September 28
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 14 - A verdict in the Rome in-absentia trial of four Egyptian national security officials charged with abducting and torturing to death Italian student Giulio Regeni in Cairo in January-February 2016 has been set for September 28, the court said Tuesday. The 28-year-old student's half-naked body was found in a ditch on the Cairo-Alexandria highway on February 3, 2016, nine days after he disappeared on the Cairo metro on January 25. Regeni, a Friuli-born Cambridge University doctoral researcher into Cairo street unions, was allegedly abducted and tortured to death by National
items.titleIt: Regeni verdict September 28

items.title: Egypt receive a warm welcome home
items.description: Watch as the Egyptian football team return to a hero's welcome from their fans.
items.titleIt: Egypt receive a warm welcome home

items.title: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab
items.description: The Somali government’s battle with Al-Shabaab militants grinds on, with neither able to gain a decisive advantage. To improve its lot, Mogadishu needs to bolster its military and alleviate civilian suffering in areas under insurgent control, while looking for openings for dialogue with the group.
items.titleIt: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab

items.title: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran
items.description: Egypt has been walking a tightrope since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, joining mediation efforts while signalling solidarity with Gulf Arab partners under Iranian attack. But with some Gulf capitals voicing expectations of stronger support, Cairo may find itself under increasing pressure.
items.titleIt: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran

items.title: Kenyan grandmothers find health, friendship and purpose through football
items.description: For these women, football is about far more than the final score. It is helping them stay healthy, reduce isolation and build confidence—showing that it's never too late to embrace sport and that ageing can be active, joyful and full of new possibilities
items.titleIt: Kenyan grandmothers find health, friendship and purpose through football

items.title: Uganda: Malaba Traffic Snarl Deepens As Ura Defends Reinstated Cargo Seals
items.description: [Nile Post] A traffic snarl stretching nearly 35 kilometres into Kenya has crippled operations at the Malaba border crossing, with truck drivers, transporters and clearing agents blaming the Uganda Revenue Authority's (URA) reinstatement of metallic electronic cargo seals for the prolonged delays.
items.titleIt: Uganda: Malaba Traffic Snarl Deepens As Ura Defends Reinstated Cargo Seals

items.title: Kenya: IEBC Fines Gikaria Sh2.5mn Over Ol Kalou Remarks, Orders Apology and Retraction
items.description: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- The Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission (IEBC) has fined Nakuru Town East Member of Parliament David Gikaria Sh2.5 million over remarks made during campaigns for the Ol Kalou by-election, ordering that the amount be paid within 48 hours of its determination.
items.titleIt: Kenya: IEBC Fines Gikaria Sh2.5mn Over Ol Kalou Remarks, Orders Apology and Retraction

items.title: Villa warned over sportswashing after Visit Rwanda deal
items.description: Aston Villa are warned their new £20m-a-year sponsorship deal with Visit Rwanda will be used by the nation to sportswash its human rights record.
items.titleIt: Villa warned over sportswashing after Visit Rwanda deal

items.title: Udine mayor probed for vote buying
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 14 - Udine Mayor Felice Alberto De Toni has been placed under investigation on suspicion of vote buying, judicial sources in the Friuli city said Tuesday. Also under investigation are opposition councilor Stefano Salmè and his partner, Daniela Perissutti, vice president of "Arriva Udine," a publicly held local and suburban public transportation company, TV 12 reported. The case stems from a complaint filed in 2024 by twelve opposition councilors, except Salmè, asking the judiciary to open investigations into two incidents: a pre-runoff agreement with Ivano Marchiol—who was a
items.titleIt: Udine mayor probed for vote buying

items.title: EU bans purchase and import of Sudanese gold in bid to curb war economy
items.description: The European Union is banning the purchase, import and transfer of Sudanese gold in a bid to curb sources of financing for the country’s devastating conflict.
items.titleIt: EU bans purchase and import of Sudanese gold in bid to curb war economy

items.title: UK-Switzerland deal to scrap roaming charges and allow Britons to use e-gates
items.description: The UK signs a new services deal with Switzerland it says will increase exports by £5.2bn annually "in the long run".
items.titleIt: UK-Switzerland deal to scrap roaming charges and allow Britons to use e-gates
